issue: dedup(items) should remove duplicate values while preserving first-seen order, and every call must be independent of previous ones. In practice the results depend on earlier calls: the first call looks correct, but calling dedup again -- even on a brand new list -- drops values that were seen during an earlier call, and can even return an empty list. The de-duplication bookkeeping is being shared across invocations instead of starting fresh each time. Make each call self-contained so identical inputs always produce identical outputs.
